name: Tabs
description: Tabbed content with keyboard nav and animated active indicator.

api.name: Tabs
api.description: Tabbed content with keyboard nav and animated active indicator.

parameters.name: AnimatedIndicator
parameters.description: When true, renders a sliding underline that animates between active triggers inside each TabsList. Default is false (no underline, original behaviour).

parameters.name: RenderMode
parameters.description: Controls when each TabsContent panel is rendered into the DOM and whether its state survives a tab switch: Active (default) — only the active panel is in the DOM; switching away unmounts it (form inputs, scroll position and any component state are lost). Lightest footprint. Lazy — a panel is rendered the first time its tab becomes active, then kept in the DOM (hidden) so its state is preserved on subsequent switches. Like DevExpress OnDemand. Eager — every panel is rendered immediately; inactive ones are hidden. State always preserved, but the heaviest initial render. Like DevExpress Always. A single TabsContent can opt into eager rendering regardless of this setting via ForceMount="true".

parameters.name: SwipeEnabled
parameters.description: When true, a horizontal pointer drag (>50 px horizontal, <30 px vertical) on any TabsContent panel switches to the next or previous tab. Default is false (opt-in).

parameters.name: SwipeWrap
parameters.description: When true, swiping past the last tab wraps around to the first (and vice-versa). Default is false (no wrap-around at boundaries).

parameters.name: Activation
parameters.description: How arrow-key navigation activates a tab (WAI-ARIA tabs pattern): Automatic (default) — arrow keys move focus AND activate the focused tab immediately (its panel shows). Best when switching tabs is cheap. Manual — arrow keys only move focus; the user presses Enter or Space (or clicks) to activate. Preferred when activating a tab is expensive (loads data, runs queries).

parameters.name: IconReveal
parameters.description: Icon-tab reveal mode: inactive triggers collapse to icon-only, and the active trigger smoothly animates its text label into view next to the icon (the label width grows from 0 → auto via a CSS grid transition, plus a fade). Lets a compact icon bar stay readable — only the current tab spells itself out. Give each TabsTrigger an IconContent (the always-visible icon) and ChildContent (the revealed label). Default false (labels always shown).

enums.name: TabsActivation
enums.description: Arrow-key activation mode for tabs. See Activation.

enums.name: TabsRenderMode
enums.description: Render strategy for TabsContent panels. See RenderMode for the semantics of each value.

records.name: TabsContext
records.signature:
TabsContext(
        string ActiveValue,
        EventCallback<string> SetValue,
        Lumeo.Orientation Orientation,
        TabsVariant Variant,
        Dictionary<string, (string TabId, string PanelId, bool Disabled)> TabIds,
        bool AnimatedIndicator,
        TabsRenderMode RenderMode,
        HashSet<string> ActivatedValues,
        bool SwipeEnabled,
        bool SwipeWrap,
        Action<string> RegisterContent,
        Action<string> UnregisterContent,
        Func<string, Task> SwipeNavigate,
        TabsReorderState Reorder,
        TabsActivation Activation = TabsActivation.Automatic)

examples.title: Default
examples.code:
<Tabs ActiveValue="account">
    <TabsList>
        <TabsTrigger Value="account">Account</TabsTrigger>
        <TabsTrigger Value="password">Password</TabsTrigger>
    </TabsList>
    <TabsContent Value="account">
        <Card>
            <CardHeader>
                <Heading Level="3" Size="lg" Class="leading-none tracking-tight">Account</Heading>
                <Lumeo.Text Size="sm" Color="muted">Make changes to your account here.</Lumeo.Text>
            </CardHeader>
            <CardContent>
                <Stack Gap="4">
                    <FormField Label="Name">
                        <Input Value="Pedro Duarte" />
                    </FormField>
                    <FormField Label="Username">
                        <Input Value="@peduarte" />
                    </FormField>
                </Stack>
            </CardContent>
            <CardFooter>
                <Button>Save changes</Button>
            </CardFooter>
        </Card>
    </TabsContent>
    <TabsContent Value="password">
        <Card>
            <CardHeader>
                <Heading Level="3" Size="lg" Class="leading-none tracking-tight">Password</Heading>
                <Lumeo.Text Size="sm" Color="muted">Change your password here.</Lumeo.Text>
            </CardHeader>
            <CardContent>
                <Stack Gap="4">
                    <FormField Label="Current password">
                        <Input type="password" />
                    </FormField>
                    <FormField Label="New password">
                        <Input type="password" />
                    </FormField>
                </Stack>
            </CardContent>
            <CardFooter>
                <Button>Save password</Button>
            </CardFooter>
        </Card>
    </TabsContent>
</Tabs>

examples.title: Icon reveal
examples.code:
<Tabs ActiveValue="members" IconReveal Variant="Tabs.TabsVariant.Card">
    <TabsList>
        <TabsTrigger Value="members">
            <IconContent><Blazicon Svg="Lucide.Users" class="h-4 w-4" /></IconContent>
            Members
        </TabsTrigger>
        <TabsTrigger Value="credentials">
            <IconContent><Blazicon Svg="Lucide.Award" class="h-4 w-4" /></IconContent>
            Shared Credentials
        </TabsTrigger>
        @* …more icon tabs… *@
    </TabsList>
    <TabsContent Value="members">Team members and their roles.</TabsContent>
    <TabsContent Value="credentials">Credentials shared with this team.</TabsContent>
</Tabs>

examples.title: Notifications
examples.code: All Unread Archived @foreach (var n in _notifications) { @n.Title @n.Time } @foreach (var n in _notifications.Where(x => x.Unread)) { /* same */ } No archived notifications.

examples.title: Vertical Tabs
examples.code:
Account Security Notifications
AccountManage your account settings. Account settings content goes here. SecurityUpdate your security preferences. Security settings content goes here. NotificationsConfigure notification preferences. Notification settings content goes here.
@code { private string _verticalTab = "account"; }

parameters.name: ForceMount
parameters.description: When true, this panel is always rendered into the DOM (hidden when inactive), regardless of the parent RenderMode. Use for panels whose state must survive every tab switch — e.g. a half-filled form — while leaving the rest of the tab set on the lighter default mode.

parameters.name: Arrows
parameters.description: When true (and Scrollable), shows chevron buttons at the start/end of the list that scroll it when the tabs overflow. The arrows auto-hide at the scroll boundaries (and entirely when nothing overflows). Default true — a scrollable list with no way to reach off-screen tabs by mouse is a usability gap (#239). Set false to keep the bare scroll/swipe affordance.

parameters.name: Reorderable
parameters.description: When true, each enabled TabsTrigger becomes draggable (HTML5 drag-and-drop on desktop, touch drag on mobile). On a successful reorder the library raises OnReorder with the from/to indices and moved value — the library does NOT mutate the order. The consumer's handler is responsible for updating the underlying collection and re-rendering.

parameters.name: OnReorder
parameters.description: Fires when the user drops a dragged TabsTrigger on a new position. Only meaningful when Reorderable is true.

parameters.name: SavedLayout
parameters.description: Optional previously-persisted tab ordering. The library does not mutate the consumer's backing collection on its own — pass this to your parent component on init and apply Order to your collection before rendering. Mirrors DataGrid's SavedLayout parameter and pairs with OnLayoutChanged to round-trip the order through your storage of choice.

parameters.name: OnLayoutChanged
parameters.description: Fires immediately after each successful reorder with the post-drop tab order, so the consumer can persist it (e.g. to local storage). The snapshot reflects the order the consumer's collection will have once it applies the corresponding OnReorder. Only meaningful when Reorderable is true. Pairs with SavedLayout to round-trip the order through storage.

events.name: OnReorder
events.description: Fires when the user drops a dragged TabsTrigger on a new position. Only meaningful when Reorderable is true.

events.name: OnLayoutChanged
events.description: Fires immediately after each successful reorder with the post-drop tab order, so the consumer can persist it (e.g. to local storage). The snapshot reflects the order the consumer's collection will have once it applies the corresponding OnReorder. Only meaningful when Reorderable is true. Pairs with SavedLayout to round-trip the order through storage.

parameters.name: IconContent
parameters.description: Icon slot. Named IconContent to avoid shadowing the <Icon> component.

slots.name: IconContent
slots.description: Icon slot. Named IconContent to avoid shadowing the <Icon> component.

mdSummary:
# Tabs

Tabbed content with keyboard nav and animated active indicator.

**Package:** `Lumeo` · **Category:** Navigation
**Docs:** https://lumeo.nativ.sh/components/tabs

## Parameters
| Owner | Name | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Tabs | ChildContent | `RenderFragment?` | `` |  |
| Tabs | ActiveValue | `string` | `""` |  |
| Tabs | ActiveValueChanged | `EventCallback<string>` | `` |  |
| Tabs | Orientation | `Lumeo.Orientation` | `Lumeo.Orientation.Horizontal` |  |
| Tabs | Variant | `TabsVariant` | `TabsVariant.Default` |  |
| Tabs | AnimatedIndicator | `bool` | `` | When true, renders a sliding underline that animates between active triggers inside each TabsList. Default is false (no underline, original behaviour). |
| Tabs | RenderMode | `TabsRenderMode` | `TabsRenderMode.Active` | Controls when each TabsContent panel is rendered into the DOM and whether its state survives a tab switch: Active (default) — only the active panel is in the DOM; switching away unmounts it (form inputs, scroll position and any component state are lost). Lightest footprint. Lazy — a panel is rendered the first time its tab becomes active, then kept in the DOM (hidden) so its state is preserved on subsequent switches. Like DevExpress OnDemand. Eager — every panel is rendered immediately; inactive ones are hidden. State always preserved, but the heaviest initial render. Like DevExpress Always. A single TabsContent can opt into eager rendering regardless of this setting via ForceMount="true". |
| Tabs | SwipeEnabled | `bool` | `` | When true, a horizontal pointer drag (>50 px horizontal, <30 px vertical) on any TabsContent panel switches to the next or previous tab. Default is false (opt-in). |
| Tabs | SwipeWrap | `bool` | `` | When true, swiping past the last tab wraps around to the first (and vice-versa). Default is false (no wrap-around at boundaries). |
| Tabs | Activation | `TabsActivation` | `TabsActivation.Automatic` | How arrow-key navigation activates a tab (WAI-ARIA tabs pattern): Automatic (default) — arrow keys move focus AND activate the focused tab immediately (its panel shows). Best when switching tabs is cheap. Manual — arrow keys only move focus; the user presses Enter or Space (or clicks) to activate. Preferred when activating a tab is expensive (loads data, runs queries). |
| Tabs | IconReveal | `bool` | `` | Icon-tab reveal mode: inactive triggers collapse to icon-only, and the active trigger smoothly animates its text label into view next to the icon (the label width grows from 0 → auto via a CSS grid transition, plus a fade). Lets a compact icon bar stay readable — only the current tab spells itself out. Give each TabsTrigger an IconContent (the always-visible icon) and ChildContent (the revealed label). Default false (labels always shown). |
| Tabs | Class | `string?` | `` |  |
| Tabs | AdditionalAttributes | `Dictionary<string, object>?` | `` |  |
| TabsContent | Context | `Tabs.TabsContext` | `default!` |  |
| TabsContent | ChildContent | `RenderFragment?` | `` |  |
| TabsContent | Value | `string` | `""` |  |
| TabsContent | ForceMount | `bool` | `` | When true, this panel is always rendered into the DOM (hidden when inactive), regardless of the parent RenderMode. Use for panels whose state must survive every tab switch — e.g. a half-filled form — while leaving the rest of the tab set on the lighter default mode. |
| TabsContent | Class | `string?` | `` |  |
| TabsContent | AdditionalAttributes | `Dictionary<string, object>?` | `` |  |
| TabsList | Context | `Tabs.TabsContext?` | `` |  |
| TabsList | ChildContent | `RenderFragment?` | `` |  |
| TabsList | TrailingContent | `RenderFragment?` | `` |  |
| TabsList | Scrollable | `bool` | `` |  |
| TabsList | Arrows | `bool` | `true` | When true (and Scrollable), shows chevron buttons at the start/end of the list that scroll it when the tabs overflow. The arrows auto-hide at the scroll boundaries (and entirely when nothing overflows). Default true — a scrollable list with no way to reach off-screen tabs by mouse is a usability gap (#239). Set false to keep the bare scroll/swipe affordance. |
| TabsList | Reorderable | `bool` | `` | When true, each enabled TabsTrigger becomes draggable (HTML5 drag-and-drop on desktop, touch drag on mobile). On a successful reorder the library raises OnReorder with the from/to indices and moved value — the library does NOT mutate the order. The consumer's handler is responsible for updating the underlying collection and re-rendering. |
| TabsList | OnReorder | `EventCallback<TabsReorderEventArgs>` | `` | Fires when the user drops a dragged TabsTrigger on a new position. Only meaningful when Reorderable is true. |
| TabsList | SavedLayout | `TabsLayout?` | `` | Optional previously-persisted tab ordering. The library does not mutate the consumer's backing collection on its own — pass this to your parent component on init and apply Order to your collection before rendering. Mirrors DataGrid's SavedLayout parameter and pairs with OnLayoutChanged to round-trip the order through your storage of choice. |
| TabsList | OnLayoutChanged | `EventCallback<TabsLayout>` | `` | Fires immediately after each successful reorder with the post-drop tab order, so the consumer can persist it (e.g. to local storage). The snapshot reflects the order the consumer's collection will have once it applies the corresponding OnReorder. Only meaningful when Reorderable is true. Pairs with SavedLayout to round-trip the order through storage. |
| TabsList | Class | `string?` | `` |  |
| TabsList | AdditionalAttributes | `Dictionary<string, object>?` | `` |  |
| TabsTrigger | Context | `Tabs.TabsContext` | `default!` |  |
| TabsTrigger | Density | `Lumeo.Density?` | `` |  |
| TabsTrigger | ChildContent | `RenderFragment?` | `` |  |
| TabsTrigger | IconContent | `RenderFragment?` | `` | Icon slot. Named IconContent to avoid shadowing the <Icon> component. |
| TabsTrigger | TrailingContent | `RenderFragment?` | `` |  |
| TabsTrigger | Value | `string` | `""` |  |
| TabsTrigger | IsClosable | `bool` | `` |  |
| TabsTrigger | Disabled | `bool` | `` |  |
| TabsTrigger | OnClose | `EventCallback` | `` |  |
| TabsTrigger | Class | `string?` | `` |  |
| TabsTrigger | AdditionalAttributes | `Dictionary<string, object>?` | `` |  |

## Slots
- `ChildContent` (Tabs) — RenderFragment slot
- `ChildContent` (TabsContent) — RenderFragment slot
- `ChildContent` (TabsList) — RenderFragment slot
- `TrailingContent` (TabsList) — RenderFragment slot
- `ChildContent` (TabsTrigger) — RenderFragment slot
- `IconContent` (TabsTrigger) — Icon slot. Named IconContent to avoid shadowing the <Icon> component.
- `TrailingContent` (TabsTrigger) — RenderFragment slot

## Events
- `ActiveValueChanged` (`EventCallback<string>`)

## Sub-components
- `TabsContent`, `TabsList`, `TabsTrigger`

## Cascading values it expects
- `Context` (`Tabs.TabsContext`)
- `Density` (`Lumeo.Density?`)

## Services it injects
- `IComponentInteropService` (Lumeo.Services)
- `ThemeService` (Lumeo.Services)
- `ILumeoLocalizer` (Lumeo.Services.Localization)
- `IComponentInteropService` ()
- `ThemeService` ()

## Keyboard
- `Delete` — HandleKeyDown handles Delete in TabsTrigger.razor
- `ArrowDown` — HandleKeyDown handles ArrowDown in TabsTrigger.razor
- `ArrowUp` — HandleKeyDown handles ArrowUp in TabsTrigger.razor
- `Home` — HandleKeyDown handles Home in TabsTrigger.razor
- `End` — HandleKeyDown handles End in TabsTrigger.razor
- `Enter` — HandleKeyDown handles Enter in TabsTrigger.razor
- ` ` — HandleKeyDown handles   in TabsTrigger.razor
- `Spacebar` — HandleKeyDown handles Spacebar in TabsTrigger.razor

## Example
```razor
<Tabs />
```

## CSS variables
`--color-background`, `--color-border`, `--color-card`, `--color-foreground`, `--color-muted`, `--color-muted-foreground`, `--color-primary`, `--color-primary-foreground`, `--color-ring`, `--radius`

- `UI/Tabs/Tabs.razor`
- `UI/Tabs/TabsContent.razor`
- `UI/Tabs/TabsLayout.cs`
- `UI/Tabs/TabsList.razor`
- `UI/Tabs/TabsReorderEventArgs.cs`
- `UI/Tabs/TabsTrigger.razor`
